page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c2d server family. It is configured with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The instance is powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, featuring 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor includes an L1d cache of 32 KB, an L2 cache of 0.5 MB, and an L3 cache of 32 MB. It supports hardware-level features such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES and VAES cryptography, SHA_NI, and hypervisor virtualization. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the c2d-highmem-8 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing it in the top 25% of tested servers. Multi-core CPU performance falls within the average range, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 11730.12 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6622.0. Memory subsystem performance is a notable strength, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25%. Uncached memory bandwidth is measured at 62.15 GB/sec for read-only large blocks and 39.95 GB/sec for write large blocks. For database and key-value operations, the instance achieves an average of 8.97 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and a Passmark database score of 3786.37.

The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory footprint per core. While it handles small-scale language model inference adequately, processing larger models exposes performance limitations, with prompt processing on a medium 7B model dropping to a weak rating of 18.65 tokens per second. The lack of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached persistent disks, which should be factored into the overall architectural design. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per core provides a resource-efficient profile for memory-bound applications without the added expense of unneeded local storage or accelerator hardware.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, delivering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 physical cores and dedicated CPU allocation, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and high memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 25% of tested systems. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall in the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 8.97 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 10.63 GB/sec. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and web serving, though it exhibits performance tradeoffs in larger LLM inference tasks and lacks local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, offering strong single-core and memory performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-8 features 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, delivering strong memory performance for database and caching workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 64 GB of RAM, providing a high 8 GB per core ratio
Strong single-core CPU and memory performance ranking in the top 25%
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and web serving
